seoTitle: How to Make a Cover Page in Google Docs (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Design a professional cover page in Google Docs with background styling, title typography, and a section break so it stays separate from your report.
intro: A cover page sets the tone for a thesis, proposal, or report before the reader even reaches the content. This guide covers structuring a dedicated cover layout in Google Docs, styling your title and background, and using a section break to isolate the cover's formatting from the rest of the document. It's a quick way to give any document a more polished first impression.

steps.heading: Create a blank cover area
steps.body: Place your cursor at the very beginning of your document to start building a dedicated cover layout separate from your body content.

steps.heading: Adjust background and margins
steps.body: Use Page Setup to customize background color and margin spacing for the cover, giving it visual distinction from the rest of the file.

steps.heading: Add title alignment structures
steps.body: Use tables or shapes from the Insert menu to build alignment containers for your title, subtitle, and author information.

steps.heading: Style your title typography
steps.body: Apply larger font sizes, weighted subtitles, and custom line-height to make your cover title stand out clearly.

steps.heading: Hide headers, footers, and page numbers
steps.body: Turn on Different first page so automated running content doesn't clutter your cover layout.

steps.heading: Lock the cover with a section break
steps.body: Insert a Section Break (Next Page) to permanently separate your cover's formatting from the document body that follows.

faq.question: How do I stop the page number from appearing on my cover page?
faq.answer: Enable the Different first page setting in your header and footer options so the cover stays free of automated numbering.

faq.question: Can I use a background image on my cover page?
faq.answer: You can insert an image and set its wrap option to behind text, effectively creating a background visual behind your title elements.

faq.question: Why should I use a section break instead of a regular page break?
faq.answer: A section break lets you control formatting, like headers and margins, independently for the cover so it doesn't affect the rest of your document.

faq.question: Is this method different for a thesis versus a business proposal?
faq.answer: The core technique is the same, though academic covers often require specific formatting rules that your institution or style guide may specify.
